title: Research: does effect polymorphism ?E interact correctly with closure captures?
description: A closure that closes over mutable state has hidden effects not tracked in its type signature. If that closure is passed to a function using ?E effect polymorphism, the ?E reflects the closure's declared effects — but not the effects of mutation through captured state. Example: a closure capturing &mut Vec has observable effects (mutation) but these are not IO, Net, or any named effect. If the effect system only tracks named effects and not mutation-through-captures, then ?E functions can silently have more effects than their signatures indicate. Research: (1) Does mutation of captured state count as an effect in Ferrum? (2) How does the borrow checker interact with the effect system for captured mutable references?
close_reason: Added 'Closure Captures and the Effect System' subsection to §2.5: mutation through captures is orthogonal to named effects, tracked by Fn/FnMut/FnOnce hierarchy not the effect system. Table showing the two systems answer different questions. No spec gap — the design is sound; the explanation was missing.
